instructions:
Standalone accuracy win surfaced by the 0080 TypeDoc pilot. Independent of the 0081 hybrid migration — this fix lands NOW so the regex extractor stops counting `@hidden`-tagged internal symbols as public API. Even if 0081 ships and supersedes this code path, this is a 1-spec correctness improvement that the validator users benefit from immediately.

Full context in [.docs-ops/evals/typescript-surface-comparison.md](.docs-ops/evals/typescript-surface-comparison.md) §3b. TypeDoc confirmed 33 currently-over-captured symbols all have `@hidden` JSDoc tags in the SDK source. They're internal API (marker sync, query helpers, paging utilities, cache primitives) — doc references to them are wrong, not just unmatched.

## The 33 confirmed @hidden symbols

**Namespaced (8 members):**
- `Client.getActiveClient`, `Client.markerSync`, `Client.markerSyncTrigger`, `Client.getMarkerSyncEvents`, `Client.setMarkerSyncEvents`, `Client.pushMarkerSyncEvent`
- `SubChannelRepository.deleteSubChannel`
- `PostRepository.deletePost`

**Root exports (25 symbols):**
`createUserToken`, `filterByChannelMembership`, `filterByCommunityMembership`, `filterByFeedType`, `filterByPostDataTypes`, `filterByPropEquality`, `filterBySearchTerm`, `getNetworkTopic`, `getSmartFeedChannelTopic`, `getSmartFeedMessageTopic`, `getSmartFeedSubChannelTopic`, `isAfterBefore`, `isAfterBeforeRaw`, `isCachable`, `isFetcher`, `isFresh`, `isLocal`, `isMutator`, `isOffline`, `isPaged`, `isSkip`, `toPage`, `toPageRaw`, `toToken`, `upsertInCache`

## Two implementation options — pick the smaller one that's correct

### Option A (preferred if straightforward): JSDoc `@hidden` tag detection

Extend the regex extractor to detect `@hidden` in the JSDoc comment immediately preceding any exported symbol declaration. Skip those during extraction. Durable across future SDK changes — new `@hidden` additions auto-honored.

### Option B (fallback if A is brittle): hardcoded exclusion set

Add a literal `HIDDEN_SYMBOLS` set to `typescript-extractor.py` listing the 33 confirmed symbols above. Filter them at the end of extraction. Less durable (new `@hidden` additions need manual maintenance) but a guaranteed-correct cleanup of what's known wrong today.

Prefer A. Fall back to B if the JSDoc parsing turns out to need substantial new logic — the goal is the cleanup, not perfect parsing.

## Steps

1. Modify `typescript-extractor.py` per the chosen option.
2. Regenerate `.docs-ops/sdk-surface/typescript.json`.
3. Verify all 33 symbols are now absent from the surface (`grep` check).
4. Run `python3 .docs-ops/ci/check-drift.py --quiet`. New drift items may appear — those are doc pages referencing internal API that should never have been documented. List them in `.docs-ops/evals/sdk-tickets-to-file.md` under a new section "TS @hidden symbol cleanup (task 0083)" with REWRITE category. Don't touch the doc pages — file the tickets and stop.
5. If 0081 has already landed by the time you start this, this task may be a no-op (the hybrid extractor already honors @hidden via TypeDoc). In that case, exit early and note in result.json.

## Out of scope

- Updating doc pages to remove references to the 33 symbols — file SDK tickets only.
- Detecting other JSDoc tags (`@deprecated`, `@internal`, `@beta`) — separate task if useful later.
- The hybrid migration (0081) supersedes this work long-term; 0083 is a near-term cleanup.

## Success criteria

- All 33 listed symbols absent from `.docs-ops/sdk-surface/typescript.json`.
- Local drift gate either passes or surfaces only `@hidden`-removal drift (which is ticketed not fixed).
- New section appended to `sdk-tickets-to-file.md` if any drift items resulted.

Report in `notes`: which option (A or B) was chosen and why; how many new drift items hit the gate; ticket section added.

validators.name: hidden_symbols_removed_from_surface
validators.command: python3 -c "import json; d=json.load(open('.docs-ops/sdk-surface/typescript.json')); root=set(e.get('name','') if isinstance(e,dict) else e for e in d.get('root_exports',[])); client_members=set(m.get('name','') if isinstance(m,dict) else m for m in d.get('namespaces',{}).get('Client',{}).get('members',[])); hidden_root={'createUserToken','filterByChannelMembership','isCachable','isFetcher','toToken','upsertInCache'}; hidden_client={'getActiveClient','markerSync','pushMarkerSyncEvent'}; root_leaks=hidden_root&root; client_leaks=hidden_client&client_members; assert not root_leaks, f'@hidden root exports still present: {root_leaks}'; assert not client_leaks, f'@hidden Client members still present: {client_leaks}'; print(f'OK @hidden symbols removed from surface')"
